content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be re-framed as hierarchical ecosystems where individual memory traces act as autonomous niche-adapting agents competing for retrieval activation based on the active learning agent’s current curiosity-driven goals. This competition prunes low-relevance memory traces naturally while prioritizing un-anchored novel information that aligns with the agent’s exploration objectives, eliminating the need for manual memory pruning protocols.
